quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Electron Configuration, Orbital Diagrams, and Isoelectronic Species
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Electron Configuration, Aufbau Principle, Orbital Diagrams, Unpaired Electrons, Valence Electrons, Isoelectronic Ions, Subshells and Orbitals

questions.question: In regard to the electronic configuration, which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: The correct Aufbau filling order is: 1s, 2s, 2p, 3s, 3p, 4s, 3d, 4p, 5s, 4d, 5p, 6s, 4f, 5d, 6p, 7s, 5f, 6d, 7p. Option A matches this exactly.

questions.question: Is anything wrong with this Aufbau diagram for Nitrogen?
options: Yes, all 2P boxes need to be filled with single arrows first
Yes, all 1s, 2s, and 2P boxes need to be filled with single arrows first
Yes, we cannot have a blank box
questions.explanation: According to Hund's rule, electrons fill degenerate orbitals singly before pairing. Nitrogen has three electrons in the 2p subshell, so each 2p orbital should contain one single arrow (all parallel spins) before any pairing occurs.

questions.question: The Aufbau diagram shown represents:
questions.explanation: Neon (Z=10) has the electron configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶. Its orbital diagram shows completely filled 1s, 2s, and 2p subshells with all electrons paired.

questions.question: What electron configuration matches an oxygen atom?
What atom matches this electron configuration? 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s²
questions.explanation: 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² totals 12 electrons, which corresponds to magnesium (Z=12).

questions.question: Which of the following species has the same electronic configuration as argon (Ar)?
questions.explanation: Argon (Ar, Z=18) has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶. Cl⁻ (Z=17+1=18 electrons) has the same configuration. Na⁺ has 10 electrons (neon configuration), Mg²⁺ has 10 electrons, and Al³⁺ has 10 electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following ions has the same electronic configuration as neon (Ne)?
questions.explanation: Neon (Ne, Z=10) has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶. O²⁻ (Z=8+2=10 electrons) has the same configuration. K⁺ has 18 electrons (argon configuration), Ca²⁺ has 18 electrons, and Cl⁻ has 18 electrons.

questions.question: The electronic configuration for aluminum is _________
The electronic configuration for carbon is __________
How many orbitals are there in the 4d subshell?
questions.explanation: All d subshells, regardless of energy level, contain 5 orbitals. The 4d subshell has 5 orbitals, each holding up to 2 electrons.

questions.question: What is the number of unpaired electrons in carbon atom?
questions.explanation: Carbon has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p². According to Hund's rule, the two 2p electrons occupy separate orbitals with parallel spins, giving 2 unpaired electrons.

questions.question: What is the number of unpaired electrons in sodium atom?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na, Z=11) has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s¹. The single 3s electron is unpaired, so sodium has 1 unpaired electron.

questions.question: What is the number of unpaired electrons in silicon atom?
questions.explanation: Silicon (Si, Z=14) has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p². The two 3p electrons occupy separate orbitals with parallel spins, giving 2 unpaired electrons.

questions.question: What is the number of unpaired electrons in sulfur atom?
questions.explanation: Sulfur (S, Z=16) has configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁴. In the 3p subshell, one orbital contains a pair and two orbitals contain single electrons, giving 2 unpaired electrons.

questions.question: Which of these elements has 4 valence electrons?
questions.explanation: Valence electrons are electrons in the outermost shell. Carbon has configuration 2, 4 → 4 valence electrons. Oxygen has 6, nitrogen has 5, and neon has 8 valence electrons.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered electron configurations and orbital diagrams! ⚛️📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review isoelectronic species and unpaired electron counts.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on Hund's rule and valence electron identification.
scoreMessages.0: Please review electron configuration notation, orbital diagrams, and isoelectronic ions.
